1 Download Ptplot 2.0 tar download zip download Ptplot1.3p1 Earlier versions Skip to sidebar Ptplot on the Zaurus Ptplot 1.0 and Ptplot 2.0 can run on the Sharp Zaurus! Set up Download http://ptolemy.eecs.berkeley.edu/java/ptolemy.plot2.0/ptolemy/plo

eecs.berkeley.edu/java/ptolemy.plot2.0/ptolemy/plot/ptolemy.plot.jar (tar file version: http://ptolemy.eecs.berkeley.edu/java/ptolemy.plot2.0/ptolemy/plot/ptolemy_plot_jar.tar ) On the Zaurus put ptolemy.plot.jar into a directory. I put it in /home/

lemy.plot.jar into a directory. I put it in /home/root/lib Create a shell script on the Zaurus at /home/root/bin/ptplot . The script should look like: #!/bin/sh # Ptplot evm -classpath /home/root/lib/ptolemy.plot.jar ptolemy.plot.PlotApplication $@ M

ptolemy.plot.jar ptolemy.plot.PlotApplication $@ Make the shell script executable chmod a+x /home/root/bin/ptplot On the Zaurus, add /home/root/bin to your path: export PATH=/home/root/bin:${PATH} Run the script ptplot The default image should come u

the script ptplot The default image should come up Create a simple data file cat > plot.plt << EoF 1 1 2 3 4 5 EoF Run ptplot: ptplot plot.plt For more information, see the Ptplot 2.0 web page at http://ptolemy.eecs.berkeley.edu/java/ptolemy.plot2.0

p://ptolemy.eecs.berkeley.edu/java/ptolemy.plot2.0/ptolemy/plot/index.html Recompiling The basic idea is to recompile Ptplot using javac -target 1.1 and then run using gevm g Versions of PtPlot after PtPlot 2.0 use Java 1.2 features or use Swing. I

PtPlot 2.0 use Java 1.2 features or use Swing. I don't think the JVM on the Zaurus can handle 1.2 or Swing. Download http://ptolemy.eecs.berkeley.edu/java/ptolemy.plot2.0/ptolemy/plot/ptolemy.plot2.0.tar.gz to a Windows or Unix box Untar, set $PTII

.tar.gz to a Windows or Unix box Untar, set $PTII and configure: cd ~/src gtar -zxf /tmp/ptolemy.plot2.0.tar.gz cd ptolemy.plot2.0 setenv PTII `pwd` ./configure Edit $PTII/mk/ptII.mk and add -target 1.1 to gJFLAGS g JFLAGS = -g -target 1.1 Note tha

.1 to gJFLAGS g JFLAGS = -g -target 1.1 Note that I had to remove -depend from the above line Run make clean make make install This will produce ptolemy.plot2.0/ptolemy/plot/ptolemy.plot.jar . Copy ptolemy.plot2.0/ptolemy/plot/ptolemy.plot.jar to t

ptolemy.plot2.0/ptolemy/plot/ptolemy.plot.jar to the Zaurus. I put it in /home/root/lib Create a small script called ptplot : #!/bin/sh # Ptplot evm -classpath /home/root/lib/ptolemy.plot.jar ptolemy.plot.PlotApplication $@ I created this script on

.plot.PlotApplication $@ I created this script on my host machine, did chmod a+x ptptot and then copied it to the zaurus to /home/root/lib/ptplot On the zaurus, run /home/root/lib/ptplot Contact Update Help Copyright © 1999 - UC Regents; all rights r
